title: Failed Authentication Ratio Trending
spl:
index=security tag=authentication action=failure OR action=success
| timechart span=1h count(eval(action="failure")) as fail count(eval(action="success")) as ok
| eval fail_ratio=round(fail/(fail+ok)*100,2)
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, _time span=1h
| xyseries _time Authentication.action count
| fillnull value=0 failure success
| eval fail_ratio=if(failure+success>0, round(failure/(failure+success)*100,2), null())
description: Rising failure ratio vs. successes indicates credential attacks, misconfigured apps, or account lockout waves ‚Äî normalized via CIM across vendors.
value: Rising failure ratio vs. successes indicates credential attacks, misconfigured apps, or account lockout waves ‚Äî normalized via CIM across vendors.
implementation: Accelerate the Authentication data model; alert when `fail_ratio` exceeds baseline + 3œÉ for the same hour-of-week.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Any TA mapping to `Authentication`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype` values tagged for CIM Authentication (e.g. `WinEventLog:Security`, `o365:audit`, `vpn:syslog`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Accelerate the Authentication data model; alert when `fail_ratio` exceeds baseline + 3œÉ for the same hour-of-week.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=authentication action=failure OR action=success
| timechart span=1h count(eval(action="failure")) as fail count(eval(action="success")) as ok
| eval fail_ratio=round(fail/(fail+ok)*100,2)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Failed Authentication Ratio Trending** ‚Äî Rising failure ratio vs. successes indicates credential attacks, misconfigured apps, or account lockout waves ‚Äî normalized via CIM across vendors.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype` values tagged for CIM Authentication (e.g. `WinEventLog:Security`, `o365:audit`, `vpn:syslog`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Any TA mapping to `Authentication`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets ‚Äî ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **fail_ratio** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, _time span=1h
| xyseries _time Authentication.action count
| fillnull value=0 failure success
| eval fail_ratio=if(failure+success>0, round(failure/(failure+success)*100,2), null())
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Failed Authentication Ratio Trending** ‚Äî Rising failure ratio vs. successes indicates credential attacks, misconfigured apps, or account lockout waves ‚Äî normalized via CIM across vendors.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype` values tagged for CIM Authentication (e.g. `WinEventLog:Security`, `o365:audit`, `vpn:syslog`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Any TA mapping to `Authentication`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Pivots fields for charting with `xyseries`.
‚Ä¢ Fills null values with `fillnull`.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **fail_ratio** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (fail vs. ok), Single value (fail ratio %).
visualization: Line chart (fail vs. ok), Single value (fail ratio %).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
